Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/REQ)

Notice And Motion To Compel Subpoena Compliance By Healthright 360 And Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Counsel N. William Metke
Off Calendar Jan-06-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Prior to Open Court, Atty. Grainne Callan, Atty. William Metke and Atty. Andrea Frey appear via Zoom. The clerk informs parties a supplemental protective order has been filed on 1/2/26. Ms. Frey states she no longer contests the court's tentative ruling. Ms. Callan and Mr. Metke states they do not contest the court's tentative ruling. The clerk releases parties.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Hilary Passman and Devil's Teeth, Inc.'s unopposed Motion To Compel Subpoena Compliance By Healthright 360 And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

Defendants move under Code of Civil procedure section 1987.1. Section 1987.1(a) provides: "If a subpoena requires the attendance of a witness or the production of books, documents, electronically stored information, or other things before a court, or at the trial of an issue therein, or at the taking of a deposition, the court, upon motion reasonably made by any person described in subdivision (b), or upon the court's own motion after giving counsel notice and an opportunity to be heard, may make an order quashing the subpoena entirely, modifying it, or directing compliance with it upon those terms or conditions as the court shall declare, including protective orders. In addition, the court may make any other order as may be appropriate to protect the person from unreasonable or oppressive demands, including unreasonable violations of the right of privacy of the person."

Good cause appearing, the court orders Healthright 360 to comply in full with Defendants' June 6, 2025 Subpoena as modified by the court's July 29, 2025 Order within 15 court days of service of notice of this order.

Defendants' request for sanctions is denied.

To the extent Defendants seek different or additional relief, the motion is denied.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Grainne Callan
Off Calendar Aug-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Grainne Callan, Esq. 408-982-6224 for Plaintiff.
N. William Metke, Esq. 415-738-6266, 415-742-4972 x306 The Mitzel Group LLP for Defendant. (In-person)

ARGUED. Ms. Callan requests the court reconsider its ruling, addresses the issues on separate statement and substantial justification and sanctions. The Court shares its views. Mr. Metke responds; sufficiency of meet and confer, discovery issue not reasonably calculated, extremely invasive, there is prejudice to Defendants. After arguments, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARIA SULAIMANI's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions.

Plaintiff Maria Francesca Sulaimani's motion to compel further responses to requests for production 59, 65, 67, 69, 71-75, 83, 84, 85, 86. 89, 90, 92 is Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an order re motion to compel discovery responses and for monetary sanctions.(see scanned order for details).***

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena For Production Of Business Records; Request For Sanctions; Declaration Of Grainne Callan
Off Calendar Jul-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Grainne Callan, Esq., of GRAINNE CALLAN, Tel:(408) 982-6224 for plaintiff. (via Zoom Conference).
N. WILLIAM METKE, Esq., of THE MITZEL GROUP, LLP., Tel: (415) 813-4984 for defendants HILARY PASMAN and DEVIL'S TEETH, INC.

Hearing held. Plaintiff counsel contests the tentative ruling regarding 1. The finding that Plaintiff failed to make a reasonable and good faith attempt at informal resolution. 2. The finding that Plaintiff treated the meet and confer obligation as an inconvenient obstacle rather than a real opportunity to resolve issues. 3. The Court's reliance on Metke Declaration paragraphs 17-18 and Exhibits 11-12 for the above findings. The Defendant's counsel contests the tentative ruling denying both parties competing sanctions requests and raises objections regarding the moving party misuse of the discovery process.
Plaintiff's counsel also requests that the matter be considered in conjunction with protective order, which was not opposed by Defendant's counsel. The matter argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARIA SULAIMANI's Motion to Quash Subpoena for Production of Business Records; Request for Sanctions. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After hearing an Order Re: MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS was signed***

Notice Of Motion And Motion To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-03-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Grainne Callan, Esq., GRAINNE CALLAN, ESQ. 408-982-6224 Attorney For Plaintiff, MARIA FRANCESCA SULAIMANI
MATTHEW ZUMSTEIN, Esq., O'HAGAN MEYER, 415-604-0162 mzumstein@ohaganmeyer.com Attorneys for Defendants HILARY PASSMAN and DEVIL'S TEETH, INC.

Discussed, not ARGUED. Counsel seeks clarification as to the last sentence in the tentative ruling re: continuance of trial. Clarification is provided by the Court.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARIA SULAIMANI'S Motion To File First Amended Complaint

Plaintiff's motion to file first amended complaint is granted. "That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89; see also CCP 428.50(c).) If a trial continuance is thought necessary, it may be sought in department 206.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
